events.title: US Strikes Iranian-Flagged Tankers
events.desc: US forces fired on and disabled two Iranian-flagged tankers that attempted to violate the American blockade of Iran's ports. A US Navy F/A-18 Super Hornet disabled both tankers after firing precision munitions into their smokestacks, preventing the non-compliant ships from entering Iran.

events.title: US Strikes Two Empty Iranian Oil Tankers
events.desc: The U.S. military struck two empty Iranian-flagged oil tankers that it said were seeking to violate the ongoing U.S. blockade. A third Iranian-flagged vessel had been disabled on Wednesday. All three vessels are no longer transiting to Iran.

events.title: Hezbollah Strikes Israeli Military Base
events.desc: Iran-backed Hezbollah launched missiles at a military base in Israel in response to a Beirut strike that killed a top commander and other attacks in the south. A parallel ceasefire in Lebanon was also under strain.

events.title: Israeli Strikes on Southern Lebanon Escalate
events.desc: Israel has increased its attacks across southern Lebanon. Israeli air raids on southern Lebanon escalate ahead of new round of talks expected next week in Washington, DC. More than a dozen reported killed.

events.title: Israel Strikes Beirut, Targeting Senior Hezbollah Figure
events.desc: Israel says it targeted a senior Hezbollah figure in the capital, Beirut. This is the first strike of its kind in the capital since mid-April, escalating tensions.

events.title: Two Waves of Bombing Hit Iranian IRGC Compound and School
events.desc: New videos geolocated and verified show deadly strikes that hit an Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) compound as well as an adjacent school in Minab in late February. The videos show multiple missiles hitting the complex.

events.title: Destruction of Iranian Police Infrastructure
events.desc: At least 80 police stations or infrastructure related to law enforcement agencies and the Basij paramilitary group have been damaged or destroyed in the first three weeks of the United States and Israel’s war against Iran, aiming to degrade the regime’s “repressive capacity”.

events.title: US Revises UN Resolution on Iran; China, Russia Expected to Veto
events.desc: Washington has revised its proposed U.N. resolution demanding Iran halt attacks and mining in the Strait of Hormuz. However, the changes are unlikely to avert Chinese and Russian vetoes, diplomats said.

events.title: Russia Emphasizes Need to Support US-Iran Talks
events.desc: Russian Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov told his UAE counterpart that U.S.-Iran talks need to be supported to prevent a resumption of hostilities in the Middle East. Russia stressed the need to focus on supporting ongoing negotiations between Iran and the USA.

events.title: Qatar PM Calls for Renewed Diplomacy with Iran
events.desc: Qatar's prime minister called Friday for renewed diplomacy on an agreement with Iran as he met in Washington with Vice President JD Vance. Discussions included Pakistani-led efforts to broker a permanent peace amid a shaky ceasefire.

events.title: Pope Leo Asks Leaders to Calm Tensions, Reduce Hatred
events.desc: Pope Leo asked that God would inspire world leaders to calm global tensions and reduce hatred, a day after meeting U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio. He asked worshippers to pray that global governments would turn away from violence.

events.title: US Expecting Iran's Response to Proposal
events.desc: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio stated Washington was expecting a response from Iran on Friday to US proposals for a deal to end the conflict. He hopes it is a 'serious offer' that can lead to a 'serious process of negotiation'.

events.title: US Pauses Operation to Guide Vessels Through Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: The US president announced a pause to 'Project Freedom', an operation to guide vessels through the Strait of Hormuz. The operation began on Monday but will be halted as progress is made toward a deal with Iran.

events.title: Israeli Military Investigates Soldier's Act Against Christian Statue in Lebanon
events.desc: The Israeli military is investigating an incident where a soldier was photographed placing a cigarette in the mouth of a statue of the Virgin Mary in Lebanon. This follows a previous incident against a Christian figure.

events.title: Libya's Largest Oil Refinery Halts Operations Amid Fighting
events.desc: Libya's largest oil refinery in Zawiya shut down operations as a precautionary measure. The shutdown occurred amidst nearby explosions and gunfire, leading to an declared emergency.
